channel.title: Game Design on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Game Design on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: AD&D Design Pillars
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Advanced Dungeons and Dragons design pillars.

item.title: Evolution Of Design Documentation
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the evolution of design documentation.

item.title: One More Game
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about making one more game.

item.title: Lenses
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about lenses.

item.title: Design Potholes
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about design potholes.

item.title: Overanalyzing Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about overanalyzing games.

item.title: Randomness In Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about randomness in games.

item.title: Toys And Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about toys and games.

item.title: Finding Meaning In Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about finding meaning in games.

item.title: What Is An Immersive Sim?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what is an immersive sim.

item.title: Temple Of Elemental Evil Design Highlights
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Temple of Elemental Evil design highlights.

item.title: Lessons From Old Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about lessons from old games I’ve never talked about.

item.title: Psychology In Video Game Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about psychology in video games because a good designer thinks about how people think.

item.title: Timelessness
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about timelessness.

item.title: Min Player Spec
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about min player spec.

item.title: Game Punishments
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game punishments, where the player does something in the game and gets punished for it.

item.title: Playing Games As Intended
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about playing games as intended.

item.title: Tiles, Walking, And Projection
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about tiles, walking and projection And just off the top, let me just say

item.title: Unneeded Realistic Designs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about unneeded realistic designs.

item.title: Traditional Game Mechanics: What I Tried
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about traditional game mechanics and what I tried to do in video games based on those mechanics.

item.title: Unpopular Game Opinions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about unpopular game opinions.

item.title: Numerical Item Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about numerical item design.

item.title: Alternate Fallout Radiation Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about an alternate Fallout radiation design.

item.title: Game Time
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game time, and by that I mean timed quests, and even more than just timed quests, and also the concept of time in a game.

item.title: My Favorite Shipped Designs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my favorite shipped designs.

item.title: "Impossible" Ideas
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about impossible ideas.

item.title: Game Length
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game length in particular.

item.title: Having Unique Rules For Players
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about having unique rules for players, and

item.title: My Ideas That Got Rejected
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my ideas that got rejected from my own games, and I’m going to start all the way back with Fallout, which was now what?

item.title: Learning Design From Travel
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about learning design from travel.

item.title: WizWar: A Study In Great Board Game Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about wiiz war, a study in great board game design.

item.title: My Games As Other Genres
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about modeling, constipation in role playing games e.

item.title: Game Design Portfolio
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about your game design portfolio or, more specifically, what you, as a game designer, should include in a portfolio that you’re sending to a game company when you apply for a job.

item.title: Fast Travel
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about fast travel.

item.title: Game Simplification
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game simplification, and by this I’m talking about various ways games can be made easier to use but to some players feel handh holdy.

item.title: Intent
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about intent, and I will get to how this refers to G video games, but I wanted to talk about it because it’s come up a lot in a lot of my videos.

item.title: Design Fallbacks
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about design fallbacks now.

item.title: Balancing Friction
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about balancing friction, and this comes from a question from padon Roso — I hope I’m pronouncing your name correctly — who asked: can you talk about balancing friction in RPGs and games?

item.title: My Games’ Theme
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my games theme.

item.title: Encumbrance, Part 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about encumbrance again.

item.title: Learning Game Design From Old Sitcoms
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about learning game design from old sitcoms.

item.title: Balancing Game Features
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about balancing game features.

item.title: Horror In Non-Horror Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about horror and non horror games.

item.title: Encumbrance
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about encumbrance.

item.title: Code The Rules, Script The Exceptions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about coding rules for your game and using scripts for the exceptions.

item.title: Content As Reward
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about content as reward.

item.title: Beyond Fighting, Sneaking, and Talking
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to brainstorm ideas with you on ways RPGs can move past the fighting, sneaking, talking Triad that, let’s face it, has been done to death since the 90s.

item.title: Gameplay Options
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about gameplay options now.

item.title: Game Flavor
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game flavor, and I’m deliberately doing this on a Friday, Funday, because what I’m going to talk about is very subjective.

item.title: Core Game Loops
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about core game Loops.

item.title: High Level Design Direction
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about highlevel design Direction.

item.title: Selling A Game Design Spec
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about selling a game design spec.

item.title: Unused GDC 1999 Roadtrip Presentation
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about RPG 2000.

item.title: Fallout As A Work Of Art
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout as a work of art, and just before I get started, I’m talking about the whole series.

item.title: Deep Learning
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about deep learning.

item.title: Learning Design From Other Disciplines
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about learning design from other disciplines, and by disciplines I can mean Hobbies, activities, anything you do besides video games.

item.title: Games As Actions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about games as actions, and I’ll talk about what I mean by that.

item.title: Understanding Game Design Choices
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about understanding game design choices.

item.title: Influential Books
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about influential books.

item.title: Bartle's Player Archetypes
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about B’s player archetypes, which is also known as the ble taxonomy of players.

item.title: Why I Hate Cultural References
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I hate cultural references.

item.title: Managing Game Expectations
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about managing game expectations, and let me explain what I mean by that.

item.title: Games Without End & New Game Plus
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game endings and new game plus — two different things.

item.title: What Is Fun?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about fun.

item.title: Designing Mechanics First
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about the idea of Designing mechanics first for your game before doing anything else.

item.title: Easter Eggs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Easter eggs specifically.

item.title: Explaining Non-Linearity
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about explaining nonlinearity and how this used to be really hard.

item.title: Game Difficulty Settings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game difficulty.

item.title: Achievements
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about achievements.

item.title: "Bad Games"
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about bad games — and I’m laughing even saying that, because I think it’s such a weird term and it can mean so many different things.

item.title: Emergent Gameplay
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about emergent gameplay.

item.title: The Four Steps For Fixing Design Problems
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about fixing design problems.

item.title: Game Design Specification Sections
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about all the different design specifications that a single game may need, because about a month ago I gave an example of how to write a design specification and gypsum generation asks: what are all the design specifications that a game might need?

item.title: Taking Games Seriously
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about taking games seriously.

item.title: Thoughts On A Temple Remake
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my thoughts on a temple remake, a remake of Temple of Elemental Evil, with things I would like to do that either I ran out of time to do or wish

item.title: Thoughts On An Arcanum Remake
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about thoughts on an Arcanum remake.

item.title: Story Vs Game Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about Story versus game design or, as sherner asks, what is more important for a game, story or game design?

item.title: Analyzing Game Designs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about analyzing games.

item.title: Writing Design Specifications
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about how to write design specifications.

item.title: Beginnings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the beginning of a game.

item.title: My Work Pattern For Designing
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my work pattern for Designing, and you can consider this a companion video to the one I did on programming, which I’ll put a link in the description.

item.title: Insignificant Features (That I Really Like)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about features that I really notice in games.

item.title: Ambiguous Grief
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about ambiguous grief.

item.title: Why I Play Games I Don't Like
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about why I play games I don’t like.

item.title: Game Pacing
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game pacing.

item.title: Fun vs. Realism
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game design: fun versus realism, and this has come up in the past.

item.title: Board Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about board games — games Charlie K 5964 asks: do you ever play board games?

item.title: Game Economy
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about game economy.

item.title: Procedural vs. Handmade Content
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about procedural versus handmade content in games.

item.title: Thoughts On A Fallout Remake
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question from Evan shantlin Who, full disclosure, worked on QA on Fallout.

item.title: Durability Mechanics
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer another question about weapon durability — ice dog 111 asks: I was wondering if you could do a video about weapon durability or equipment durability as a game mechanic.

item.title: Creeping Normality
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about creeping normality.

item.title: Game Designs That Impressed Me
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer another question, good one from octopus RPG who asks: I’m curious if there are any games that you didn’t work on with mechanics or design that made you think — quote: wow, I wish

item.title: Hedonic Adaptation
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk to you about hedonic adaptation.

item.title: Speedrunning
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to ask and answer another question from Tommy salami6694, who asks: hey, Tim, I was wondering if speedrunning has any effect on the games you’ve made, specifically the scope of bug fixes, post launch and internal developer views on speedrunning.

item.title: Player Engagement
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about player engagement.

item.title: Games As Art (and thoughts on the Fallout TV Show)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about games as art and the meaning of games, but it’s going to be a little rambling because I’m going to tell you how

item.title: My Development Preferences
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my development preferences.

item.title: What Makes A Good Game
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what makes a good game.

item.title: Fallout: What I'd Change Today
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what changes I would make to Fallout if I were to Tinker with it today.

item.title: Design Pillars
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about design pillars.

item.title: Non-Linear Game Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about nonlinear game design.

item.title: Challenges Facing Game Designers
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what it’s like to be a computer game designer — I - I originally was going to say what it was like to also be a programmer and an artist, even though the art one I pretty much watched, seen what they go through.

item.title: How To Write Design Docs
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim. I’ve told a lot of stories about game development, but today I actually want to talk about process, specifically how I make my design documents.
